California
Fair Political
SUPERSEDED
Practices Commission
bg l?'7oz.t (a ) (4)
December 20, L99L
ceorge Basye
Downey, Brand, Seymour and Rohwer
555 capitoL MaII, 10th Floor
Sacramento, cA 9 5814 -4 68 5
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
our FiIe No. I-91-495
Dear Mr. Basye:
This is in response to your Ietter requesting advice on
behalf of the board of trustees of Reclamation District No' 1004
concerning their duties under the conf l ict -of - interest provisions
of the Political 
Reform Act (the "Act").1 
Since you have not
provided the names of the public officials 
on whose behalf you
have requested advice, we are treating your request as one for
informal assistance.2
provide the requestor with the
or forrnal written advice. (Section
OUESTI ON
May the trustees of Reclamation District No. 1oo4 particj.pate
in the selection of a neutral arbitrator to nake a decision in
which the trustees have a conflict of interest?
CONCLUS I ON
So long as there is no prior knowtedge of which conclusion
the neutral arbitrator will- reach on the decision, the trustees
may participate in the decision to select the arbitrator.
1 co,r"..r*"nt code sections 81ooo-91015. AII statutory references
are to the covernment code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission
reguJ.ations appear at 2 california code of ReguJ,ations Section
18ooo, et seq. AII references to regulations are to Title 2.
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2 rnformal assistance does not
immunity provided by an opinion
83114,' Regulation 18329 (c) (3) . )
428 J Street, Suite 8OO a P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958o4-OBo7 . (9t6)322-566o


Our File No. I-91-495
Page 2
FACTS
In 1991, Reclamation District No. 1004 (the "districtr')
participated in the state Emergency Drought Water Bank (the
"program") . 
Hov,rever, due to a niscalculationr the district 
ended
the year hrith a 5400,000 deficiency, The issue before the
district 
is whether the deficiency and resultant loss should be
allocated soIeIy to the well water users v/ho participated in the
program, or lrhether the deficiency should be alLocated to aIl
L'ater users in the district.
The district's 
governing board of trustees is composed of
five mernbers. Two of the district's 
trustees participated in the
program. Three of the trustees did not participate in the
program. Thus, depending on which option is selected, the
trustees r.rilI be financially affected in different manners.
You stated in your letter that you believed the trustees had
a conflict of interest. 
In our telephone conversation of Decenber
4, 1991, you clarified that your specific question concerns the
potential selection of an arbitrator by the board to make the
a l,location decis ion.
ANALYSI S
Section 87100 of the Act prohibits any public offj.cial from
making, participating in making, or otherwise using his or her
official 
position to influence a governnental decision in which
the official 
has a financial interest.
Section 87103 provides that an officiaL r.rill have a financial
interest in a decision vrithin the meaning of Section 87100 if it
is reasonably foreseeable that the decision wiII have a naterial
financial effect, distinguishable frorn its effect on the public
generalJ,y, on the official 
or a member of his or her immediate
family. 
Regulation La'|02- 1(a) (4) provides a decision will have a
material financial effect on an official 
where the decision wil]
result in the personal expenses, income, assets, or liabilities 
of
the official 
increasing or decreasing by $250. (Torrance Advice
Letter, No. I-89-142. )
You stated that you bel j.eve the trustees have a conflict of
interest rrith respect to the method that the district's 
deficit is
paid. If the allocation is lj.nited to the rrell water users who
participated in the program, the two trustees that did participate
in the program vrould end up paying a hj.gher portion of the
deficit, 
and the three trustees r"/ho dj.d not participate in the
program r,rould pay nothing. Conversel.y, if the deficit is
allocated unifornly to all water users in the district, 
the two
trustees that participated in the progran would pay less, while
the other three trustees would have to pay a portion.


You have asked $rhether the trustees may vote to select an
arbitrator to nake the decision concerning ho$, the burden of
paying the deficit is distributed.3 
In the Dorsev Advice Letter,
No. A-eg-gg6, we advised that a councilmember who resigned from
the city's redevelopment agency due to conflicts of interest
caused ty ownership of real property within the project area could
participate in the decision to appoint a replacernent provided that
tne apptintment decision would not have a foreseeable, material
financial effect on the official. 
we concluded that absent a
specific agreement bet$reen the councilmember and his replacement
tirat the litter 
would vote in a partlcular manner, it was not
foreseeable that the appointment would affect the counci lmernber' s
financial interests. 
(see aIso, Lofqren Advice Letter, No. A-86-
307, )
According to your facts, the decision before the board of
trustees is whether to bring in a neutral arbitrator to nake the
decision for which your trustees are financially interested.
Consistent with the Past advice described above, it is not
reasonably foreseeable that the selection of an nuetral arbitrator
v/i.11 affeat the interests of any of the trustees so long as there
is no prior knowledge of which way the arbitrator wiII decide the
particular decision. of course, once an arbitrator is selected,
the trustees may not use their official 
position to attempt to
influence the decision of the arbitrator. 
(section 87100;
Regulation 18700. r. )
I trust that this ansWers your questions.

Our File No. I-91-495
Page 3
If you should need
feel free to
Sincerely,
Scott
HaI Iabrin
General Counsel
Act i ng
hllace
LegaI Division

with a conflict of
interest may participate in the making of a governmental decision

official 
is legally required to participate in a governmental
decision onl,v if there exists no alternati.ve source of decision'

in the selection of a neutral arbitrator to nake a decision in

CONCLUSION: So long as there is no prior knowtedge of which conclusion
the neutral arbitrator will- reach on the decision, the trustees
may participate in the decision to select the arbitrator.
